name: Vegetable-Stuffed Meat Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small skillet, cook shredded carrots and potatoes in hot oil until tender, stirring occasionally.
2. In a medium mixing bowl, combine beaten egg and milk. Stir in bread crumbs, parsley, onion salt, garlic powder, and pepper.
3. Add ground beef and mix well to form the meat mixture.
4. Crisscross three 18x2-inch foil strips atop a sheet of waxed paper.
5. In the center of the foil strips, pat half of the meat mixture into a 5-inch circle on another sheet of waxed paper.
6. Spread carrot mixture over the meat circle, leaving about 1/2 inch from the edges.
7. Pat remaining meat mixture into a 6-inch circle on waxed paper and invert it atop the first circle.
8. Remove the waxed paper and press the edges together to seal well.
9. Bring up the foil strips and transfer the meat loaf to a crockery cooker, leaving the foil under the meat during cooking.
10. Press the meat away from the sides of the cooker. Cover and cook on low-heat for 9½-11½ hours or on high-heat for 3½-4 hours.
11. In a bowl, combine catsup and mustard. Spread over the meat loaf.
12. Cover and continue cooking on low-heat or high-heat setting for an additional 30 minutes.
13. Using the foil strips, transfer the cooked meat loaf to a platter; discard the foil strips.
